svg.title: PC hardware milestones that reshaped the industry, 1981 to 2026
svg.desc: Timeline showing eight key hardware milestones from the IBM PC in 1981 through Apple Silicon in 2020, each one a before-and-after moment for personal computing.

g.text: Establishes the architecture defining personal computing for 40+ years
32-bit protected mode unlocks real operating systems and multitasking
Hardware stops being a config nightmare; PCs become consumer-friendly
First universal peripheral standard since the IBM PC itself
Breaks the 4 GB memory ceiling; enables virtualization and the cloud era
Clock speed scaling ends; parallel computing becomes mandatory
The 30-year storage bottleneck breaks; responsiveness transforms
The x86 monopoly cracks; ARM becomes a viable PC architecture
